label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 4

extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ Primož attacked inside final kilometer on punchy finish, gapped PEDERSEN Mads for victory and red jersey lead.
tactical_patterns: ELISSONDE Kenny set tempo into final km positioning ROGLIČ, who launched late after PEDERSEN committed to earlier acceleration.
BENNETT Sam's slow puncture removal from finale proved crucial—reduced sprint points at line protected his green jersey despite absence.
ROGLIČ collected mountain points before finish sprint, revealing multi-classification strategy when stage win uncertain.
PEDERSEN Mads beat BENNETT at intermediate sprint narrowing green jersey gap, showing awareness of points opportunity on reduced-sprint stage.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Timed acceleration perfectly after letting PEDERSEN move first, showing tactical patience superior to raw power display
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: Strong second on climbing sprint finish reveals expanded stage-winning range beyond pure flat sprints
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: Slow puncture decision to change wheel cost stage points but preserved green jersey through earlier intermediate sprint
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: Battled SIVAKOV for podium on punchy finish, indicating climbing form beyond mountain stages
rider_signals.SIVAKOV Pavel: positive: Fifth place on climbing sprint shows unexpected punch, potential for harder finishes than typical mountain domestique
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: Finished well on climbing sprint without being mentioned in finale positioning, suggesting reserved energy management
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: positive: Recovered to defend young rider jersey on climbing finish despite commentary suggesting earlier difficulty
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Contested mountain points pre-finish showing classification awareness but lacked punch in final sprint
